service: TunnelMind — x402 v1 payment surface discovery

paid_endpoints.purpose: x402 demo endpoint — proves the HTTP-402 challenge → X-PAYMENT retry → settlement-response wire works end-to-end against TunnelMind. Echoes the request body on valid payment.

demo_mode.purpose: Validate your x402 client implementation without moving any USDC. The HMAC verifier accepts a nonce + SHA-256-HMAC pair signed against a Worker-side secret. Settlement is simulated; the response is identical-shape to real-mode.

construction.step_1: Generate a 16+ character random nonce (UUIDv4 works).
construction.step_2: Compute HMAC-SHA256(secret=<see notes>, message=`x402-demo-echo:` + nonce). Hex-lowercase the digest.
construction.step_3: Build payment payload: { x402Version: 1, mode: 'demo', nonce: <nonce>, hmac: <hex_digest> }.
construction.step_4: Base64-encode the JSON payload and set as X-PAYMENT header.
construction.step_5: Retry the request with the X-PAYMENT header. 200 + echo body + X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE confirms success.

notes: The demo HMAC secret is a Worker-side environment variable (X402_DEMO_HMAC). For the public smoke, the operator publishes the secret directly here — there is nothing to protect; the demo asserts only protocol correctness.
If a different secret is provisioned per-tenant in the future, this field will move to a per-key well-known doc.

real_mode.summary: The endpoint accepts mode='x402' payment payloads and forwards them to env.X402_FACILITATOR_URL (e.g., Coinbase) for verification. Activation gated on (a) TM-controlled Base USDC receive address, (b) Coinbase facilitator API key. See roadmap item #65 progress.

policy.summary: Every existing TunnelMind endpoint remains free or auth-gated as before. x402 is an additional rail for new paid surfaces; no endpoint is being moved behind x402.
policy.principle: x402 is the agent rail. Stripe is the human rail. Both rails accept the same paid quota.
